entity: Discharge Instructions: -IBUPROFEN (the ingredient of Advil, Motrin, etc.) may be taken even though you may also be taking Tylenol/Acetaminophen. You may alternate these medications for pain control.
description: This instruction allows the use of ibuprofen and acetaminophen for pain management, suggesting a strategy to manage post-operative discomfort.

entity: Discharge Instructions: -Ibuprofen should always be taken with food. Please discontinue taking and notify your doctor should you develop blood in your stool (dark, tarry stools)
description: These instructions provide guidance on the use of ibuprofen and the need to report any gastrointestinal bleeding.

entity: Discharge Instructions: -You MAY be discharged home with a medication called PYRIDIUM that will help with the "burning" pain you may experience when voiding. This medication may turn your urine bright orange.
description: Pyridium is an antispasmodic prescribed to manage urinary tract symptoms, potentially related to ureteral stent passage.

entity: Discharge Instructions: -Colace has been prescribed to avoid post surgical constipation and constipation related to narcotic pain medication. Discontinue if loose stool or diarrhea develops. Colace is a stool softener, NOT a laxative, and available over the counter.
description: Colace (docusate sodium) is prescribed as a stool softener to prevent post-surgical constipation.

entity: Discharge Instructions: -Do not eat constipating foods for weeks, drink plenty of fluids to keep hydrated. -No vigorous physical activity or sports for 4 weeks and while Foley catheter is in place.
description: These instructions provide dietary and activity restrictions post-discharge, emphasizing the need for hydration and avoiding strenuous activities.

entity: Discharge Instructions: -If YOU ARE DISCHARGED HOME WITH A FOLEY CATHETER: -Please refer to the provided nursing instructions and handout on Foley catheter care, waste elimination and leg bag usage.
description: This instruction provides guidance for patients with a Foley catheter, detailing its care and use.

entity: Discharge Instructions: -Your Foley should be secured to the catheter secure on your thigh at ALL times until your follow up with the surgeon. -Follow up in 1 week for wound check and Foley removal.
description: These instructions outline specific care and follow-up requirements for a patient with an indwelling Foley catheter.

entity: Discharge Instructions: -DO NOT have anyone else other than your Surgeon remove your Foley for any reason. -Wear Large Foley bag for majority of time, leg bag is only for short-term when leaving house.
description: These instructions emphasize the importance of proper catheter care and removal by a surgeon.
This refers to the overall set of post-discharge instructions, indicating comprehensive guidance for patient recovery and follow-up care.
This is the primary diagnosis indicating chronic issues with the mitral valve and kidney function.
CAD stands for Coronary Artery Disease, a secondary condition noted in the discharge summary.
Hypercholesterolemia is noted as a secondary condition, suggesting elevated cholesterol levels.
IDDM stands for Insulin-Dependent Diabetes Mellitus, another secondary diagnosis.
GERD is listed as a secondary condition, indicating issues with the esophagus and stomach.
